blocks.body: Future Perfect Continuous: Questions and Short Answers — Will + subject + have been + V-ing? and How long will ... have been ...?

{"level":"B2","intro":"In Future Perfect Continuous questions, we put will before the subject and then use have been + V-ing.","rules":[{"label":"Yes/No question","color":"emerald","text":"Start with Will + subject + have been + V-ing?","example":"Will you have been working long by 6 p.m.?"},{"label":"Wh-question","color":"blue","text":"The question word comes before will.","example":"What will she have been doing by then?"},{"label":"How long","color":"amber","text":"This is a very natural question when we want the duration before a future moment.","example":"How long will they have been living there by next June?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"violet","text":"In short answers, use only will or won't.","example":"Yes, I will. / No, they won't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic patterns","intro":"Look at the main types of questions and short answers.","items":[{"label":"Yes/No","title":"Will + subject + have been + V-ing?","subtitle":"Will they have been waiting long by then?"},{"label":"Wh-","title":"What / Why / Where + will + subject + have been + V-ing?","subtitle":"What will you have been doing all morning?"},{"label":"How long","title":"How long will + subject + have been + V-ing?","subtitle":"How long will she have been studying by Friday?"},{"label":"Short answer","title":"Yes, subject + will. / No, subject + won't.","subtitle":"Yes, she will. / No, they won't."}]}

{"title":"2. When these questions sound natural","sections":[{"label":"Questions about future progress","color":"emerald","description":"Ask whether a process will still be continuing at a certain future moment.","examples":[{"en":"Will you have been working by 8 p.m.?","ua":"Will your work still be in progress by eight?"},{"en":"Will she have been waiting long by then?","ua":"Will the waiting already be long by that point?"}]},{"label":"Questions about duration before an event","color":"blue","description":"These questions help you ask how long an action will already have been continuing by another event.","examples":[{"en":"How long will they have been travelling by the time they arrive?","ua":"How long will the journey already have lasted by their arrival?"},{"en":"How long will you have been teaching here by next summer?","ua":"We ask about accumulated duration before a future limit."}]},{"label":"Questions about plans and process","color":"amber","description":"Wh-questions work well when you want to ask what process will be happening before a certain moment.","examples":[{"en":"What will she have been working on by then?","ua":"What exactly will still have been going on by that time?"},{"en":"Why will he have been staying there for so long by next month?","ua":"We ask about the reason for a long duration."}]},{"label":"Short answers","color":"violet","description":"In short answers, do not repeat the whole structure unless you need extra emphasis.","examples":[{"en":"Will he have been driving long by noon? — Yes, he will.","ua":"A short answer with will."},{"en":"Will they have been studying for two hours by then? — No, they won't.","ua":"A short answer with won't."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick question patterns","intro":"Here are the most common models.","rows":[{"en":"Will you have been working long by 6 p.m.?","ua":"yes/no question","note":"a question about a process before a future hour"},{"en":"What will she have been doing all morning by then?","ua":"wh-question","note":"a question about the process itself"},{"en":"How long will they have been living there by next June?","ua":"how long question","note":"a question about accumulated duration"},{"en":"Will he have been driving for three hours by noon? — No, he won't.","ua":"short answer","note":"a short answer without repeating the whole form"}],"warning":"In short answers, we usually do not say Yes, I will have been unless the sentence continues after that."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out been in the question.","wrong":"❌ Will she have working there for a year by July?","right":"✅ Will she have been working there for a year by July?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using V1 instead of V-ing.","wrong":"❌ How long will they have been live there by next June?","right":"✅ How long will they have been living there by next June?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using a wrong short answer.","wrong":"❌ Yes, she will have been.","right":"✅ Yes, she will."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Questions in Future Perfect Continuous = Will + subject + have been + V-ing?","Questions with How long are very natural for duration before a future limit.","Put the wh-word before will.","In short answers, will or won't is enough."]}

{"title":"Future Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-questions","current":true},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/future-perfect-continuous/future-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
